title: Bridge Essay: Brain Networks, Neurotransmitters and Psychedelics: Towards a Neurochemistry of

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through Brain, Networks,, Neurotransmitters, and, Psychedelics:.

**Brain Networks, Neurotransmitters and Psychedelics: Towards a Neurochemistry of Self-Awareness.** — Curr Neurol Neurosci Rep. PMID: 38980658. DOI: 10.1007/s11910-024-01353-y. Self-awareness can be defined as the capacity of becoming the object of one'sown awareness and, increasingly, it has been the target of scientific inquiry. Self-awareness has important clinical implications, and abetter understanding of the neurochemical basis of self-awareness may help clarifying causes and developing interventions for different psychopathological conditions. The current article explores the relationship between neurochemistry and self-awareness, with special attention to the
